title: phaiophleps biflora subsp. lyckholmii
intro: Phaiophleps biflora subsp. lyckholmii, also known as Lyckholm's Phaiophleps, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Great Lakes region of North America. It is a type of orchid, characterized by its unique, plicate leaves and showy, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Phaiophleps biflora subsp. lyckholmii are said to include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, although further research is needed to confirm these claims, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves.
